items.headline: Carlton's Elijah Hollands admitted to hospital
items.description: Carlton player Elijah Hollands has been admitted to hospital four days after what his club described as "a mental health episode" against Collingwood.

items.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Barcelona step up interest in Italy right back
items.description: Barcelona are stepping up their pursuit of Atalanta right back Marco Palestra, while Arsenal remain interested in Julián Álvarez.

items.headline: Cherki caper, Haaland power help Man City flatten Arsenal: MOTW
items.description: Man City flattened Arsenal to put another intriguing twist in a Premier League title race that promises to go down to the wire.

items.headline: AFL apologises after Butters charge thrown out
items.description: The AFL has issued an apology after a tribunal panel member's decision to disconnect from Zak Butters's umpire abuse hearing then continue listening while driving led to the charge being thrown out.

items.description: How early could Jeremiyah Love be drafted? Will Ty Simpson go in Round 1? Could we still see A.J. Brown traded? Adam Schefter has the latest.

items.description: Jordan Reid projected every single pick for this week's NFL draft, from the Raiders' selection at No. 1 to 'Mr. Irrelevant.'

items.headline: Alvaro Arbeloa on LaLiga refs: 'Easier for Real Madrid to win the Champions League'
items.description: Álvaro Arbeloa said on Monday that "it's easier for Real Madrid to win the Champions League than LaLiga."

items.headline: Why Buccaneers GM Jason Licht says 'this draft is important'
items.description: After missing the playoffs in 2025, Tampa Bay's general manager knows this offseason could have long-term ramifications.

items.headline: Barry Cable acquitted of child sex abuse charges
items.description: Former footballer Barry Cable has been acquitted of a slew of historical child sexual abuse charges despite a judge finding the alleged victim was likely telling the truth.
